name: Axiogenesis
summary: Generates the baryon asymmetry from the rotation of the QCD axion (or PQ-charged complex scalar) in the early universe. PQ symmetry is explicitly broken by higher-dimensional operators or thermal effects, inducing rotation; the rotational asymmetry of the PQ charge is then converted to baryon asymmetry via QCD and electroweak sphaleron transitions. Connects axion DM and BAU through the same dynamical field.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 10^-10 for axion decay constants f_a ~ 10^8 - 10^11 GeV with explicit PQ-breaking operators. Predicts a smaller-than-canonical f_a window and connects to LHC searches for new PQ-charged states.
Same axion field that drives BAU is the DM candidate; abundance follows from misalignment + kinetic-misalignment mechanisms.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicts a narrower f_a window than vanilla axion DM; ADMX-class haloscopes sensitive.

new_fields: PQ-charged complex scalar with explicit-breaking operators
introduces.note: Builds on PQ axion. New parameters: PQ-breaking operator coefficients, initial-condition phase.

history: Co and Harigaya (2019), 'Axiogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 111602 (2020), arXiv:1910.02080. Active model-building and phenomenology 2020-present, including lepto-axiogenesis and heavy-QCD-axion variants.

references.citation: Co and Harigaya, 'Axiogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 111602 (2020)
